description_long: This is a rectangular stone block with a bas-relief carving of a bird, likely a heron or stork, on its front face. The carving is shallow but detailed, showing the bird in profile with its head, beak, and body clearly defined. The block has a rough, slightly irregular texture on its sides and back, suggesting it was hand-chiseled or weathered over time. The back of the block is flat and unadorned, while the sides are also rough and unfinished. The stone appears dense and solid, with a muted gray coloration throughout. This type of block could serve as a decorative or commemorative element in an urban park or historical site.
description: A rectangular gray stone block featuring a bas-relief carving of a bird in profile on its front face, with rough, unfinished sides and back.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed bas-relief carving of a bird, which is the main decorative feature of the block.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the block's thickness and rough, unfinished stone texture, with no visible carvings.
description_view_2: The back view displays a flat, unadorned stone surface, lacking any carvings or markings.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing the block's thickness and rough, unfinished texture.
attribute_location_context: urban park or historical plaza as a decorative or commemorative feature
attribute_installation_method: set on a prepared stone or concrete base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Slight surface roughness and minor edge wear suggest some weathering, but no significant erosion or moss growth is visible.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: A bas-relief carving of a bird is present on the front face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: appears isolated or freestanding, likely intended as a standalone decorative element
